importantInfo.title: Important Information Before Applying
items: You need to stay in Paraguay for at least one week to complete the residency process. During your stay in Asunción, we will meet in three different locations for official formalities.

Required documents: Passport, extra ID, Birth Certificate, Clean Criminal Record (all apostilled; the criminal record must be issued within the last 6 months and ideally valid for at least 3 more months).
Once you obtain your Paraguayan RUC (Tax ID), we will teach you how to legally issue invoices to your clients.
Please note: we provide guidance, but we do not issue invoices on your behalf.
Due to high demand, RUC inscription is currently taking approximately one additional month beyond the residency process.
Visit Paraguay once per year to maintain your Temporary Residency.

We are professional accountants graduated from the National University of Asunción (UNA), one of Paraguay's top institutions, and we specialize in tax strategies, including official Binding Consultations that may provide significant financial advantages.
importantInfo.eligibilityText: Want to know if you are eligible? Check Paraguay's official visa exemption list to confirm whether your country allows visa-free entry:

result.title: Your Tax Structure in Paraguay

irpWorkingInsideParaguay.name: IRP (Income Tax) - Working inside Paraguay
irpWorkingInsideParaguay.description: Progressive income tax working inside Paraguay
irpWorkingInsideParaguay.note: If you work outside Paraguay for a foreign company, income tax (IRP) IS EXEMPT

irpWorkingOutsideParaguay.name: IRP (Income Tax) - Working outside Paraguay
irpWorkingOutsideParaguay.description: Income earned from work performed outside Paraguay for a foreign company is exempt from IRP (not taxable in Paraguay)
irpWorkingOutsideParaguay.note: If you work outside Paraguay for a foreign company, income tax (IRP) IS EXEMPT

foreignSourceIncome.noObligations: No tax obligations in Paraguay for foreign-source income
foreignSourceIncome.note: Income that was generated and already taxed abroad is exempt in Paraguay. When you bring that money into Paraguay, you pay 0% tax - it's considered foreign-source income under Paraguayan tax law.

irp.name: IRP (Income Tax)
irp.details: Progressive rates: 8% (up to 50M GS), 9% (50M-150M GS), 10% (>150M GS)
irp.note: If your annual income exceeds 80 million PYG, you must register for the IRP.

irpExempt.name: IRP (Income Tax)
irpExempt.description: Exempt when working for foreign companies
irpExempt.note: If you work outside Paraguay for a foreign company, income tax (IRP) IS EXEMPT

ireSimple.name: IRE Simple (Income Tax)
ireSimple.note: If income exceeds 2 billion GS, financial statements (IRE) are required

iduResident.name: IDU (Dividend Tax) - Residents
iduResident.note: When each shareholder withdraws their profits. It doesn't matter if it's physical or legal.

iduNonResident.name: IDU (Dividend Tax) - Non-Residents
iduNonResident.description: Tax when non-resident shareholders withdraw profits
iduNonResident.note: When each shareholder withdraws their profits. It doesn't matter if it's physical or legal.

obligations.financialStatements: Financial statements if income > 2 billion GS

notes.exportExempt: If you are working outside Paraguay and you still working for a Foreign Company the INCOME TAX (IRP) IS EXEMPT TOO.
notes.cryptoVATExempt: Crypto sales are exempt from VAT.
notes.iduOnlyDistribution: IDU: When each shareholder withdraws their profits. It doesn't matter if it's physical or legal.
notes.easRequirements: To Establish the EAS: It can be a single partner or more, Legal Representative must have an ID, You need a Company Seal, There is no minimum capital, Capital should not be declared
notes.ireSwitchThreshold: If income exceeds 2 billion GS in a fiscal year, you must pay through the General IRE (Financial statements are required)
notes.capitalNotDeclared: Capital should not be declared in the EAS

result.disclaimer: *Disclaimer: Tax situations can vary based on individual circumstances. For personalized advice, please consult with a tax professional.
